articles.title: Spurs Win to Move Into Western Conference Finals
articles.original_title: Spurs win to move into Western Conference finals
articles.summary: Stephon Castle led the San Antonio Spurs to a decisive victory over the Minnesota Timberwolves, clinching the series and securing a spot in the Western Conference Finals. The win marked the Spurs' first conference‑final appearance since 2017.
key_points: Stephon Castle was the standout performer for San Antonio.
The Spurs defeated the Timberwolves in a series sweep to advance.
This is the franchise's first Western Conference Finals berth in nine years.

articles.title: Spurs Blow Out Wolves to Advance to Western Conference Finals
articles.original_title: Spurs blow out Wolves to advance to Western Conference Finals
articles.summary: San Antonio routed Minnesota, earning a place in the Western Conference Finals for the first time since 2017. The dominant performance highlighted the Spurs' resurgence in the postseason.
key_points: Spurs posted a large margin of victory over the Timberwolves.
The win ends a nine‑year drought without a conference‑final appearance.
San Antonio's postseason momentum is now focused on the upcoming series.

articles.title: NBA MVP Announcement Set for Sunday Ahead of Western Conference Finals
articles.original_title: The NBA's MVP will be announced Sunday. Gilgeous-Alexander, Wembanyama or Jokic the finalists
articles.summary: The league will announce the MVP on Sunday, the day before Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals between the Oklahoma City Thunder and the San Antonio Spurs. Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander, Victor Wembanyama, and Nikola Jokić are the three finalists.
key_points: MVP finalists: Shai Gilgeous‑Alexander (Thunder), Victor Wembanyama (Spurs), Nikola Jokić (Nuggets).
The announcement will occur on the same day as the start of the conference‑final series.
Gilgeous‑Alexander is the defending MVP and a key player for the Thunder.

articles.title: Spurs Beat Timberwolves to Advance to Western Conference Finals vs. Thunder
articles.original_title: Spurs beat Timberwolves to advance to Wester Conference finals vs. Thunder
articles.summary: Stephon Castle scored 32 points as Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs powered past Minnesota 139‑109, clinching the series in six games. The victory sets up a Western Conference Finals matchup against the Oklahoma City Thunder.
key_points: Castle contributed 32 points in the decisive Game 6.
Spurs won the series 4‑2, finishing with a 139‑109 win.
Next opponent: Oklahoma City Thunder in the conference finals.

articles.title: Donovan Mitchell Struggles in Game 6 as Pistons Defender Shuts Down Star
articles.original_title: Donovan Mitchell's Game 6 struggles continue with Cavs star getting shut down by particular Pistons defender
articles.summary: In Game 6 of the series against Detroit, Donovan Mitchell continued his streak of six consecutive Game 6 losses, facing a tough defensive effort that limited his production. The Cavaliers' star was unable to find his usual rhythm against a focused Pistons defender.
key_points: Mitchell has now lost six straight Game 6 matchups.
A specific Detroit defender significantly limited Mitchell's scoring.
The Cavaliers' chances to advance were further diminished by the offensive slowdown.

articles.title: Stephon Castle gives the Spurs 32 points in a 139-109 blowout to eliminate Timberwolves in Game 6
articles.original_title: Stephon Castle gives the Spurs 32 points in a 139-109 blowout to eliminate Timberwolves in Game 6
articles.summary: Stephon Castle had 32 points and 11 rebounds to highlight another dominant performance from the San Antonio backcourt, and Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs raced past the Minnesota Timberwolves 139-109 on Friday night to win the series in six games.

articles.title: Pistons dominate 2nd half to beat Cavs and force Game 7
articles.original_title: Pistons dominate 2nd half to beat Cavs and force Game 7
articles.summary: Cade Cunningham scored 21 points and the top-seeded Detroit Pistons dominated the second half, beating the Cleveland Cavaliers 115-94 on Friday night to force a Game 7 in their Eastern Conference second-round series.  The decisive game is Sunday in Detroit.  Jalen Duren had 15 points and 11 rebounds while Daniss Jenkins also scored 15 for the Pistons, who have won four games this postseason when facing elimination.
